entry.title: Implementing Row-Level Security using ABAC in SAP Databricks
entry.content:
In modern enterprise landscapes, especially in SAP-driven ecosystems, data security is not just about who can access a table—it’s about which rows a user is allowed to see.

Traditional Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) often falls short when:

  • Data spans multiple domains (Finance, HR, Sales)
  • Access depends on business attributes (department, region, sensitivity)
  • Governance must scale across hundreds of tables

Image generated using AI Core - Gemini Flash Lite Model

This is where Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in SAP Databricks Unity Catalog becomes powerful.

In this blog, we will:

  • Understand ABAC-based Row-Level Security (RLS)
  • Compare it with classic approaches
  • Implement it step-by-step
  • Apply it to an SAP dictionary dataset (like MARA, KNA1, etc.)

What is ABAC in SAP Databricks?

ABAC allows access decisions based on:

  • Attributes of data (tags like department, sensitivity)
  • Attributes of users (group membership)
  • Policies that connect them

Instead of hardcoding rules per table, you define:

Tags + UDF + Policy = Dynamic Row-Level Security

Architecture Overview

ABAC has three main components:

ComponentPurpose
Governed TagsClassify data
Row Filter UDFsDefine access logic
PoliciesBind logic to data

Sample Scenario: Business Requirement

Let’s take an example.

Tables (SAP Dictionary-like)

  • ZSALES_ORDER (similar to VBAK)
  • ZCUSTOMER (similar to KNA1)
  • ZEMPLOYEE (HR data)

Requirement

RoleAccess
Sales TeamOnly sales data for their region
Finance TeamOnly finance-related records
HR AdminsFull access
ExecutivesAll data

Step 1: Create Governed Tags

Tags represent metadata similar to SAP data classification fields.

CREATE TAG sensitivity VALUES ('high', 'medium', 'low');
CREATE TAG department VALUES ('sales', 'finance', 'hr');
CREATE TAG region VALUES ('EMEA', 'APAC', 'NA');

Step 2: Tag SAP Tables

Apply tags to simulate SAP dictionary semantics.

ALTER TABLE main.sap.zsales_order
SET TAGS ('department'='sales', 'region'='EMEA');

ALTER TABLE main.sap.zcustomer 
SET TAGS ('sensitivity'='high', 'department'='finance');

ALTER TABLE main.sap.zemployee 
SET TAGS ('sensitivity'='high', 'department'='hr');

Step 3: Create Row Filter UDF

This is where business logic resides.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION main.sap.filter_by_department(dept STRING, region STRING)
RETURNS BOOLEAN
RETURN
  CASE
    WHEN is_account_group_member('Executives') THEN TRUE
    WHEN is_account_group_member('HR_Admins') THEN TRUE
    WHEN is_account_group_member('Finance_Team') AND dept = 'finance' THEN TRUE
    WHEN is_account_group_member('Sales_Team') AND dept = 'sales' 
         AND region = current_user_region() THEN TRUE
    ELSE FALSE
  END;

Step 4: Create ABAC Policy

Now bind everything together.

CREATE POLICY sap_data_access_policy
ON CATALOG main
COMMENT 'Enterprise-wide SAP data access control'
ROW FILTER main.sap.filter_by_department
TO `All Users` EXCEPT `Data_Admins`, `Executives`

FOR TABLES
WHEN has_tag_value('sensitivity', 'high')

MATCH COLUMNS has_tag('department') AS dept,
              has_tag('region') AS region

USING COLUMNS (dept, region);

What Happens at Runtime?

When someone queries:

SELECT * FROM main.sap.zsales_order;

Unity Catalog executes:

  1. Checks applicable policies
  2. Validates tags (department, region)
  3. Extracts tagged columns
  4. Passes values into UDF
  5. Evaluates user group via is_account_group_member()
  6. Filters rows dynamically

When to Use ABAC

Use ABAC if:

  • You have multiple SAP datasets
  • Data is shared across domains
  • Governance needs to scale
  • You integrate SAP + SAP Datasphere / Databricks

entry.title: Re: Asset posting
entry.content:
In SAP FI, standard postings only allow one transaction type per line item. To post an asset involving two different transaction types (e.g., a concurrent acquisition and retirement, or an asset transfer), you must split the transaction. Use the Manual Posting (AB01L) or Asset Transfer (ABT1N / ABUMN) screens

How to Post Using Multiple Transaction Types
  1. Access the Transaction: Use transaction code AB01L (Post Asset Document) or ABUMN (Asset Transfer within Company Code). 
  2. Enter Header Details: Input your Document Date, Posting Date, and Company Code. []
  3. First Transaction Type (Line Item 1):
    • Enter the Posting Key (e.g., 70 for Asset Debit).
    • Enter the Account/Asset Number.
    • Enter the Transaction Type (e.g., 100 for External Acquisition).
    • Input the amount and press Enter. 
  4. Second Transaction Type (Line Item 2):
    • Enter the Posting Key (e.g., 75 for Asset Credit).
    • Enter the Account/Asset Number or the offsetting clearing account.
    • Enter the Second Transaction Type (e.g., 200 for Retirement).
    • Input the amount and press Enter.Simulate and Post: Review the document, ensure the balances match, and click Save.
    •  
      1. Simulate and Post: Review the document, ensure the balances match, and click Save.

entry.title: Help regarding SAP Business One integration
entry.content:
Hey guys,

I'm new to SAP Business One so treat me like a noob. I have a COM based tool and I want to create an integration with SAP Business One client application for UI automation. I have been trying a few things, but I'm stuck and not able to connect to the SAP Business One application itself. Just a note that I've not built the exe using the Visual Studio extension, but regular 64-bit build.

I've created an add-on using the extension generator tool (ard file + exe), but for some reason it only registers under lightweight extension in browser and shows incompatible while directly registering in the application itself. I'd like to know what difference would both make in terms of add-on?
I'd also like to understand if attaching to SAP Business One process would be better or connecting to it using connection string is recommended? Or is there any particular scenarios where each one work work? I've recently come across the following threads talking about connecting to SAP Business One using connection string but unable to understand much:
Solved: confused about connection string - SAP Community
Connecting to the UI API | SAP Help Portal
AddonIdentifier Property - SAP B1 SDK Help
I mean same connection string would work for any application? Is it? And what does AddonIdentifier() do exactly? The setup and connection links on the AddonIdentifier property page are not wokring. Is this approach as a whole fine for lightweight extensions registered in the browser or some other scenario?
So yeah, need help with these things. Whatever you know, please let me know. Any and all pieces of information are welcome. Thanks a lot guys.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA – SAP Note 1489799 for BAPI_PR_CREATE (Subcontracting PR without BOM)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

We are implementing a custom solution in SAP S/4HANA where Purchase Requisitions are created programmatically using BAPI_PR_CREATE.

Our business requirement is to create a Subcontracting Purchase Requisition (Item Category L) without a BOM.

During testing, we receive the following error:

ME154 – Creating subcontracting purchase requisition without BOM

We found SAP Note 1489799, which appears to address this issue. Before implementing the note, I would appreciate feedback from anyone who has experience with it in an SAP S/4HANA environment.

Specifically:

  1. Does SAP Note 1489799 completely resolve the ME154 error when using BAPI_PR_CREATE?
  2. After implementing the note, is it possible to successfully create a subcontracting PR without a BOM using the standard BAPI?
  3. Does the note introduce any functional or technical side effects in Materials Management (MM) or Financial Accounting (FI)?
  4. Are there any known impacts on the standard Purchase Requisition or Purchase Order creation process?
  5. Are there any successor SAP Notes or additional Notes that should be implemented together with 1489799?

Any feedback or implementation experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help.

Good day @sternams_08 

The 2608 documentation is currently provided as a preview which means an upgrade is being prepared, so some documentations may still be completed or refined before the final release documentation is published.

Should you have any challenges with your implementation, feel free to post your specific question to the community.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: CDS view Product classes and characterstics
entry.content:
Good day @Egbert_de_Jong 

Thank you for your question. Your concern is surely valid. 

This is not only your system. Your screenshot shows that I_ClfnObjectCharcValForKeyDate is released, but it is not enabled for data extraction. These are two different release attributes.

A CDS view can be released for reuse in custom CDS views, analyticy, or other supported consumption scenarios without being released for replication through Cloud Data Integration. Note that supported capabilities are assigned individually to each CDS view and determine the intended consumption scenarios.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/6a6ff32b25dd473080e6aeddbefecfca.html?locale=en-US

For product or material classification, SAP provides the following released classification views:

  • Characteristic Valuation
  • CDS Views for Classification System

However, they are generic classification and there are no information that they extraction is enabled. In comparison, dedicated extraction views for equipment and functional location characteristics values are provided via: Functional Location Character Values Data Extraction

The difference is, extraction enablement requires SAP to provide a stable extraction contract, suitable keys, performance handling and where applicable, delta capability. A released view does not automatically meet those requirements. So, I am not aware of any rule published by SAP why one business object is prioritised over another.

The current options you have is as follows:
For limited volumes, use the released classification views to created a custom CDS view and expose it through supported OData consumption scenario. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/6a6ff32b25dd473080e6aeddbefecfca.html?locale=en-US

  • Should you have recurring high-volume, consider replication into Datasphere or another data platform.

 

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: While Defining Multilevel PO Release Strategy, Boxes to Check Release Prerequisite are Missing.
entry.content:
Subject: Release Strategy Prerequisites – Checkboxes Not Displaying for Level 2 Release Strategy

Hello Everyone,

I have encountered an issue while configuring a multilevel Release Strategy in SAP and would appreciate your guidance.

Configuration Details
Purchase Organizations: 2
Release Levels: 4 for each Purchase Organization
Total Release Strategies: 16
Total Release Codes: 12

The release codes are:
10, 20, 30, ..., 90, 11, 21, 31

The release strategy is planned as follows:

RS1: Release Code 10
RS2: Release Codes 10, 50
RS3: Release Codes 10, 50, 70
RS4: Release Codes 10, 50, 70, 31

The remaining release strategies will follow the same pattern, with only the release codes changing.

Issue

I have successfully configured RS1, and it is triggering correctly for Purchase Orders.

However, while configuring RS2, after entering the release codes 10 and 50 and navigating to the Prerequisites tab, I do not see the expected checkboxes. The screen is completely blank (as shown in the attached screenshot).

Additionally, in the Release Simulation, the release codes are displayed in a single line instead of the expected checkbox layout.

Could anyone please advise:

Why the prerequisite checkboxes are not appearing?
Is there any configuration step that I might have missed?
How can I make the prerequisite checkboxes visible?

Your guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Compensation Promotion with Position Management Not Enabled in Non-EC environment
entry.content:
Hello cpmensation experts (and @xavierlegarrec)

I came here with a couple of questions regarding the compensation promotion process in Non-EC environment where position Management is not enabled.

1. First of all I need to know, if in this scenario is it possible to change employee's current position with a new one along with job family/level/code and pay grade? if possible, how to configure that?

2. In case it is possible, new position should be created in advance before making a position change, right? 

3. How to enable "promote" link in worksheet emp actions menu? (includeJobSelector and related parameteres are set to "true" in xml)

currently I access promotion fields via Employee actions --> open profile - show promotion:

Thanks for your support

entry.title: Re: BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 error when replicating Business P...
entry.content:
Good day @clebercarvalho 

Thank you for your question.

The error BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 is most likely related to the sender business system identification or your BP key mapping, not to an additional user authorisation in Public Cloud.

The communication user is already authorised through the active communication arrangement SAP_COM_0008. For inbound and outbound BP replication, SAP_COM_008 is the communication scenario used. See : APIs for Master Data Maintenance for more insights.

  • You need to verify the Business System value in your communication system. It must match the sender system identifier contained in the inbound SOAP payload. Make sure you don’t validate only the communication system name or host. In the failed message, inspect the payload and compare the sender system ID with: Communication Systems --> your Sales Cloud system --> Business System. If there is a mismatch, it can prevent S4H from assigning the inbound message to the correct source system.
  • Make sure to check your iFlow externalised parameters and confirm that the deployed iFlow uses the correct: Sales Cloud sender system ID, Receiver endpoint from the active SAP_COM_008 arrangement, Communication arrangement user and authentication method, Tenant specific system aliases. See the current integration guide for more details.
  • If your BP was originally replicated from S4H to Sales Cloud and is now returned, you must preserve the original external ID and UUID. Incorrect or duplicate key mappings can cause inbound BP replication errors. See KBA 3477813 for more details. Also compare the failed message with a successfully replicated BP, especially: 
    1. BusinessPartnerInternalID,
    2. UUID
    3. Sender business System
    4. Receiver business system
    5. Replcation task such as create or updated
    6. Address UUID or external address ID
  • Verify that every BP role, country, region, language, relationship role, and address usage sent from Sales Cloud has a valid target value. See Sales Cloud Version 2 Integration Guide for more details.

In Message Monitoring Overview, open the failed inbound message, enable the detailed trace level, record the complete long text for BP_DATAREPL_FND/006, compare the sender system in the payload with the Business System field in the communication system. Then ensure you correct the mismatch or mapping and choose Reprocess. 

 

Let me know if you have further question.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Blog 2 - Accelerating SAP BW Modernization: Hands on Experiences with the BDC Data Product Gener
entry.content: @torsten_wirth you are right in all angles. 1, DPG requires BW to stay alive, so both BW and Datasphere will be used at the same time. Also, BW Bridge has no roadmap. The vision is to use DPG to move from BW to Data Products (BDC) "at customer pace"

entry.title: Re: CDS view Product classes and characterstics
entry.content: As always thanks a lot for your explanations @Chris1973 we do a wide suite of SAP products from the SAP Ecosystem. Datasphere is 1 of them. We do follow the pattern you describe but instead of each client individuallly taking this effort here i see the added value of SAP doing this centrally with the Clean core philisophy. I know dataproducts are being worked but still disappointed about these CDS views. Better speed this up before spending more efforts in Joule marketing. As after all - Joule needs clean/structured/stanardized/interoperable data. Now instead of wildgrowth in ABAP we get wildgrowth in CDS view setups. Hope critical cds views like these are available in next monthly cycle right or even better - in 2608

entry.title: Re: Output Determination Issue (Dispatch Time)
entry.content:
What I see is that output is to be triggered only once sould be set.

Check whether you have "Multiple issuing" selected. If it is not seleceted then you may have this issue.

To resolve select "Multiple issuing" checkbox and try.

NACE and select application and click Output types to list the relevant outputs.

Then exit and try to issue the second output.

 

Regards

PKP

entry.title: Re: SAP Event Mesh for Beginners – Part 1: Event-Driven Architecture, Topics, Queues and Initial Set
entry.content: @YashikaYTD  : Thanks for comments, updated it with alignment.

entry.title: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a business requirement to create a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation.

Business Requirement - Business requires a two-step put away process:

  1. Move the HU from the source bin to an Interim Bin
  2. Move the HU from the Interim Bin to the PSA

Instead of creating the Warehouse Task directly to the PSA, the stock should first be staged in the interim location. The destination bin is changed from the PSA to the Interim Bin using the CHBD exception code during Warehouse Task confirmation.

The requirement is to automatically create a second HU Warehouse Task that moves the HU from the Interim Bin to the PSA immediately after the first Warehouse Task is confirmed.

Approaches Evaluated :

  1. POSC / LOSC - We evaluated both POSC and LOSC. However, due to business and operational constraints, these approaches are not suitable for our process.
  1. Pick Point (Pick HU) Process - We configured the Interim Bin as a Pick Point. The system correctly creates two Warehouse Tasks (Product WT and HU WT). However, after business review, this solution was rejected because of several operational limitations and it is not considered practical for day-to-day warehouse execution.

Technical Options Explored :

Option A – BADI -  /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST

This BADI is triggered during HU Warehouse Task confirmation.

The issue is that at this point, the Warehouse Task confirmation status has not yet been committed to the database. We considered calling the creation logic in an Update Task, but creating WH Task in an Update Task appears to be a heavy processing activity and is generally not recommended.

Option B – PPF

We looked for a PPF Action Profile but could not find any standard PPF action that executes after WT/WHO confirmation.

Option C – Asynchronous Background Process

Schedule a custom background job after confirmation that executes logic similar to the /SCWM/ADHU API.

Although technically feasible, the drawback is the delay between Warehouse Task confirmation and creation of the follow-on Warehouse Task. Ideally, the second HU Warehouse Task should be created immediately.

Has anyone implemented a similar requirement?

Is there any standard enhancement point, framework, or recommended approach to automatically create a follow-on HU Warehouse Task immediately after Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order confirmation, without relying on POSC/LOSC or asynchronous background processing?

Any suggestions or implementation experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

SAP Extended Warehouse Management 

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Fetch SOAP Fault Message in Receiver in CPI
entry.content:
Reply Now

 
 
Hi,
For fetching the actual SOAP faultXML body in CPI (not just theexception message), the key is toenable "Return HTTP Error Responses"on the SOAP receiver adapter. Thismakes CPI treat the 500 response asa regular message instead of throwingan exception — so it flows into youriFlow normally and you can processthe fault XML in a Message Mapping step.
Steps:1. In your SOAP receiver adapter,   check "Return HTTP Error Responses"2. The fault XML will now appear in   the message body even on 500 errors3. Use a Router to check   ${header.CamelHttpResponseCode}   = 500 and route to your error flow4. The message body at that point   contains the raw SOAP fault XML
Once you have the raw fault XML,you might find this free tool usefulfor understanding exactly what eachfault means and how to handle it: sentinel-av.in/tools/fault-parser/ 
Paste the fault XML and get aspecific diagnosis and suggestedfix instantly. No signup needed.
Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: 创建了项目参数文件：有收入的项目后，对项目进行报工，并进行了作业的实际成本率的计算后，在运行实际成本核算后，无法将作业的实际成本计入到项目中，希望得到您的帮助和支持？
entry.content: 感谢您的帮助与支持， 1.项目投资参数为有收入的项目/间接费用项目； 2.项目报工有具体的数量，有咨询类似的活动类型 3.用实际成本率计算-成本中心APP计算，得出了管理成本率-实际价格； 4.但是活动类型的计划成本率与实际成本率的差异无法进入到项目成本中， Thank you for your help and support, 1. The project investment parameters are revenue projects/overhead projects; 2. There are a specific number of project applications, and there are similar types of consulting activities. 3. Using the actual cost rate calculation-cost center APP calculation, the management cost rate-actual price is obtained; 4. However, the difference between the planned cost rate and the actual cost rate of the activity type cannot enter the project cost.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the Human Capital Management Learning Group
entry.content:
Hello,

I would recommend starting with: Explore SAP SuccessFactors Solutions (HRSF1).  After that, if you are interested in Customer focused classes, I would move to SAP SuccessFactors Platform Administration (HR800) then into the product specific courses.  If you are interested in the Consultant curriculum look at SAP SuccessFactors Platform Introduction Academy (THR80) then into the Product specific Academies.

entry.title: dead links
entry.content:
I found a lot of links on this community page are dead

SAP Activate | SAP Community

 

Example:


Explore Phase

  • Conduct a Fit-to-Standard analysis to review the functionality of SAP S/4HANA and identify configuration
  • Take advantage of process diagrams and test scripts for your Fit-to-Standard
  • Confirm phase completion with the quality-gate checklist and requesting your quality system
  • Select appropriate data migration objects and prepare for data loads
  • Plan for the execution of change management

 

And many more.
Is there a more up to date site ?

entry.title: Re: Blog#2: How Cross Landscape Distribution (XLD) can be integrated into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: @PClaus , Does pony also have feature for customizing synchronization between s4hana and mdg using cloud alm without solution manager?

entry.title: RPL
entry.content:
We are experiencing a significant data volume increase in our Reprocessing List (RPL) due to a combination of automatic backflush failures, pending standard process steps (GRIN and Usage Decisions), and continuous low-value consumable items. We need an optimal SAP solution or best-practice configuration to streamline this process.

  • Automatic Backflush Issues: An automatic backflush job runs every 3 hours. While many parts post automatically, a significant number still fail and require manual posting by the team.

  • GRIN & Quality (UD) Bottlenecks: The RPL is currently cluttered with the following:

    • Items pending Goods Receipt (GRIN).

    • Quality inspection items pending a Usage Decision (UD).

    • Because these items remain in the list until cleared, the RPL is growing excessively large.

  • Consumables and Small Parts: Continuous parts like shims, spacers, washers, and other consumables constantly appear in the RPL, requiring continuous manual resolution.

Questions / Solutions Needed

  1. How can we prevent low-value consumables (shims, washers, spacers) from hitting the RPL? Is there a way to exclude these specific material types or storage locations from the reprocessing logic (e.g., bulk material indicator or backflush exclusion)?

  2. How can we better manage or isolate GRIN and quality (UD) pending items so they do not bloat the main operational RPL?

  3. What are the best practices to reduce the failure rate of the 3-hourly automatic backflush job to minimise manual intervention?

entry.title: Re: Movement type 687 hitting BSX transaction key for Bot...
entry.content:
Hi @uday20 ,
Movement type 687 is hard‑coded by SAP to use BSX on both debit and credit. It does not support a separate transaction key for stock‑in‑transit. To post SIT correctly, SAP requires using transaction key AUM (or GBB/VAX, depending on scenario) via account modification — not via movement‑type‑level transaction key assignment. Movement type 687 = “Goods issue reversal from stock in transit” (used in STO scenarios).

687 is treated as an inventory revaluation / adjustment movement.
SAP always posts:

  • BSX credit (inventory decrease)
  • BSX debit (inventory increase)

Because the movement is considered a reclassification, SAP does not call a separate SIT transaction key.

Let me know did you get what you were looking for.

entry.title: Re: Individual MRP - Check unrestricted stock in individu...
entry.content:
Hi @Jimesh_Makwana ,
Yes — you can make Individual MRP (Strategy 50 / 60 / 70) consider unrestricted stock for commonly used raw materials.
You achieve this by removing Individual Requirements for those specific raw materials using MRP Group or Material Master settings.

In Individual MRP (Requirements Type KE, Strategy 50/60/70):
All components inherit Requirement Type = Individual
This forces:
No consumption of unrestricted stock
No consumption of planned independent requirements
No sharing between sales orders

Always creates Sales‑Order–specific PR/PO, So SAP ignores unrestricted stock by design. But SAP gives you a way to override this per material.

Use MRP Group (Best Practice for EPC), Create an MRP Group (e.g., ZBULK)
SPRO → Production → MRP → Planning → MRP Groups

In the MRP Group settings, Set Ind./Coll. Requirements = 2 (Collective Requirements), Assign this MRP Group to the raw materials.

The Result is, These raw materials will consume unrestricted stock even in an MTO BOM.

Hope you got the answer for your question.

entry.title: Variable Pay Bonus worksheet - Negative record in the employee history- Incorrect finalPayout Calc
entry.content: We have a requirement where Bonus needs to be reduced when employee is taking STD and other unpaid time (in eligible wages) for which there is no LOA or any other field being tracked in EC side. While reading the History records from EC, all assignments are eligible because there is no status change and hence, we tried to create a negative value record for the ineligible wages in the history file which calculates the overall bonus target accurately by reducing the amount and also business goal is being applied correctly and individual preview also is correct. The spent budget after IPM is also correct but the finalPayout is incorrect. We have the Bonus calc set as Base * Business Performance * Individual Performance only. Has anyone experienced/tried negatives basis in the history file for such situation to reduce bonus?

entry.title: Re: Beyond Joule Capabilities: A Knowledge Graph Driven Runtime for SAP Business AI
entry.content:
One thing I particularly appreciated is the explicit note that "knowledge graph" is being used loosely here. That level of candor is refreshing, and it's a distinction that often gets lost in current AI discussions.

To me, the really interesting contribution isn't the storage model, but the runtime pattern itself: resolving intent against the stored representation and composing OData dynamically, without per-use-case authoring. That's a compelling architectural direction in its own right.

Whether the underlying representation eventually evolves into a graph of typed relationships or stays closer to a validated pattern store doesn't diminish that idea — it simply highlights that "knowledge graph" now spans a much broader range of implementations than it did a few years ago.

The naming will sort itself out; the pattern is the part worth watching.

entry.title: Re: I was unable to select the text from quick selection...
entry.content:
Check the note 3763555 - THere is a recent issue due to browser upgrade. Probably thats the one impacting. This is my first guess.

if this is not the case then provide me the system version and what type of app (SAPUI5/WDA/GUI for HTML) having this issue?

Regards,

Mahe

entry.title: Decimal of USD in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
My customer has purchasing price in USD with 3 decimals. For example: 0.025 USD/PC.

When I input in Purchasing info record, it will turn into 0.03USD/PC.

How should I do with this case? It seems that can not change the decimals setting in SAP.

Please help. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content:
Hi @SHIVENDRA;

Really appreciate the feedback! The empty-result hallucination risk is something I plan to explore further, good reminder that a POC and a production-ready agent are very different things. Great to know others are building in this space too!

entry.title: Re: Need to Schedule a Crystal Report
entry.content:
Hello,

Crystal Reports Designer is a tool for creating reports on your data from a single User  point of view.

To be able to schedule, export, view, send to users via E-mail etc. what you require is another Application by SAP called Crystal Reports Server. It's what you want, depending on your user count depends on which version.

CR Server is a single Server Deployment for small to medium number of user and loads, for a full deployment across multiple Servers what you want is the full version of CRS called Business Object Enterprise (BOE).

To start with go here for CR Server:

https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/crystal-server.html

Last time I checked it was about $1000 and comes with a 1 year support contract. Sales will have more details...

There is a 30 day trial version available also.

To get more info on BOE go here:

https://support.sap.com/en/index.html?search=1475602

It does require a Support Contract to access but on the right side there is a Contact link to discuss your options. I don't believe there is a free trial for this version.

There is also the ability to Create your own scheduling and other functions using the CR/BOE SDK's, you will require a Developer to write the application for you using .NET, Java or Restful SDK's.

Licensing is based on your use, internal is free for your use, external requires each deployment to purchase a copy of Crystal Reports, OEM options are available also.

Also note, CR Server comes with a 1 year support Contract, BOE requires a support contract, pricing and user details etc. can be discussed with Sales.

There are also third party applications out there to do what you want, those you'll have to search for them.

For more info have a look at the FAQ site:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/crystal-reports/faq

Hope that helps...

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 16 - Escaped names
Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: Not able to find the default Event Mesh in Entitlemen...
entry.content:
AFAIK there is 3 versions of it, make sure to chose the right one for your use case.

- (Basic) Event Mesh: A standalone event mesh that needs an entitlement
- Integration Suite Event Mesh: You enable this one from Integration Suite with the PI_Admin role
- Advanced Event Mesh: Seperate product, non-SAP but pretty awesome.

entry.title: Re: Need to Schedule a Crystal Report
entry.content: Ken Hamady maintains a listing of 3rd-party Crystal Reports schedulers here.

entry.title: Re: Implementing Credit Check in Service Orders for Enhanced Financial Control
entry.content:
Hello sanju

I read your great blog. At the moment we are implementing S/4 Hana Service on Premise in the Version 2023 FPS3.

I have done all settings, but when we create Service Orders in the Fiori App Manage Service Orders, then the Customizing is ignored. When I run the creditlimit-check in the BP, all settings were checked

I found a BAdI CRMS4_SERV_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE. I implemented this in an enhancement, but the method IF_CRMS4_SERV_CHCK_BEFORE_SAVE~CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE is ignored as well (I was not able to debug it, it has not stopped).

Can you give me a hint?

Regards

Sigi Weber

entry.title: SAP Document AI Workspace: Features You Need to Know
entry.content:
This blog is a comprehensive guide to navigating the Workspace UI — from document uploads and schema configuration to channels, workflows, and transport management.

The SAP Document AI Workspace is the new, full-featured environment for all document processing tasks on SAP BTP. From now on, all the latest SAP Document AI features are available exclusively in the Workspace UI — making it the recommended starting point for every new project involving document extraction, validation, and automation.

What Can You Do in the Workspace?

The SAP Document AI Workspace handles a wide range of activities throughout the document extraction lifecycle. It provides a centralized interface that reduces operational complexity, enhances process transparency, and accelerates deployment of document AI solutions across enterprise environments.

 

👤User Menu

The User Menu in the top-right corner of the Workspace lets you personalize your experience. From here you can change the display language (34 languages are supported), adjust UI theme options, and switch between client configurations.

💡 Tip: The Workspace supports localization in over 34 languages, making it accessible for global enterprise rollouts without additional configuration.

 

📂All Documents

The All Documents screen is your central hub for managing every document uploaded to the Workspace. It supports viewing, filtering, sorting, editing, and exporting, giving you full visibility across the extraction lifecycle.

Documents move through statuses: PENDING → PROCESSING → DONE (or flagged for manual review). Once confirmed, extracted data can be exported or pushed downstream to connected SAP systems.

🧩 Schemas

Schemas are at the heart of SAP Document AI. They define which fields to extract from a given document type — both header-level fields and line-item fields. The Workspace lets you view, enable, create, configure, and activate schemas.

SAP provides pre-trained schemas for common business documents including invoices, purchase orders, remittance advices, and more. For anything beyond these, you can build custom schemas leveraging Generative AI and LLMs — no template maintenance required.

✨ Instant Learning: As users confirm or correct extractions in the Workspace, the model learns immediately via few-shot prompting — improving accuracy on the next run without a separate training cycle.

 

📡Channels

The Channels feature manages how documents enter and leave the Workspace automatically. Inbound channels can monitor email inboxes (including Microsoft Exchange/Outlook via Azure app registration), while outbound channels handle notifications and API callbacks to downstream systems.

  • Inbound Email Channel: Register an Azure app with Mail.ReadWrite permissions, then map a specific email folder to a target schema in the Workspace.
  • Automatic Ingestion: When a document arrives in the monitored inbox, the Workspace extracts it according to the configured schema — no manual upload needed.
  • Outbound Callbacks: Configure webhook endpoints to push confirmed extraction results directly to SAP S/4HANA, SAP Build Process Automation, or any external system.

⚠️ Note: Email channel configuration requires an Azure AD application registration with appropriate mail permissions and a trust setup in your SAP BTP subaccount.

⚙️Workflows

Workflows let you automate complex, multi-step document processing tasks by chaining together the core Workspace capabilities. They can be triggered automatically via inbound channels — such as incoming emails — or invoked programmatically via the OData V4 API.

For example, a workflow can automatically extract data from an uploaded income statement, pass it to SAP Build Process Automation for business-rule evaluation, and create a Sales Order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud — all without manual intervention.


 


🚀Transports

The Transports feature integrates the Workspace with SAP Cloud Transport Management, enabling you to move schemas, workflows, and channel configurations across environments (development → test → production) in a governed, traceable way.

Schemas and model versions are first-class transport artifacts: they are versioned, can be activated or rolled back independently, and are fully transportable — reducing risk when promoting changes to production.

 

🛠Settings

The Settings menu contains configuration options that apply globally across all Workspace features. Key settings include:

⭐Best Practices & Key Takeaways

  • Use the Workspace UI for all new projects. The classic Basic UI will not receive new features going forward — all innovation is Workspace-only.
  • Subscribe using the -ias application plan when setting up entitlements in SAP BTP, as this provisions the Workspace interface.
  • Document quality matters. Invest in good scanning practices — OCR accuracy and extraction quality are directly tied to input file quality.
  • Design for human-in-the-loop. Even with high confidence scores, include a review step for business-critical data.
  • Leverage Instant Learning. Confirm or correct documents regularly to continuously improve extraction accuracy without retraining.
  • Use Transports for lifecycle management. Never configure production schemas manually — use the transport pipeline.

entry.title: Re: SAP project management
entry.content: You can start here:https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4dd8cb7b1c484b4b93af84d00f60fdb8/1ad4b65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: SAP CPI OData V4 POST – Invalid CSRF Token Error for WarehouseTask Confirm API (EWM A2X)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working with the standard SAP EWM API – Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), specifically using the WarehouseTask entity. This is an OData V4 API.

🔹 Scenario

I am trying to perform a POST operation to confirm a Warehouse Task using the below endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask/EU20/1234563436/0/SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskHndlgUnit

Where:

  • EU20 → EWM Warehouse
  • 1234563436 → Warehouse Task
  • 0 → Warehouse Task Item

🔹 Approach Followed

  1. First, I executed a GET call on the same API to:

    • Fetch the CSRF token
    • Retrieve the corresponding ETag for the specific combination (Warehouse, WarehouseTask, WarehouseTaskItem)in the body.

  2. For the POST request:

    • I used the same CSRF token in the header
    • Set the header:
      If-Match = <ETag (from GET response)>

🔹 Issue Faced

While triggering the POST request, I am receiving the following error:

Invalid CSRF token

🔹 Observed Behavior

  • In the CPI trace logs (receiver side), I can see that the CSRF token is being passed in the request header.
  • However, after the failure:
    • In the request headers attachment, the CSRF token appears as "fetch" instead of the actual token value.

This is creating a confusing situation, as the token seems to be passed but still results in an "Invalid CSRF token" error.

🔹 API Reference

For reference, I am using the API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub:
Warehouse Task Confirm API

🔹 Ask

Has anyone encountered a similar issue while working with this API or OData V4 CSRF handling in CPI?

Any guidance on:

  • Correct CSRF handling approach, or
  • Possible reasons why the token is getting replaced with "fetch"
    would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

I live in smaller city where companies with SAP just do not exist. I wonder is it kind of huge blockade for me or people use to work remotely with such a roles. I do have house here etc. I'm 29 also.

entry.title: Re: Cannot access Hands-on Practice System for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core Academy

entry.title: Building a CDS Downstream Dependency Analyzer in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Introduction

When working with larger CDS models, it is very common to deal with deeply layered views where one CDS consumes several others, which in turn depend on additional CDS views. While SAP provides tools like dependency graphs in ADT, they are not always practical for detailed analysis.

In particular, a recurring challenge is:

How do we quickly identify only the custom CDS dependencies that are actually consumed downstream?

The dependency graph in ADT:

  • Displays both standard and custom CDS views
  • Is not easily exportable for further analysis

Because of this, extracting meaningful insights—especially when focusing only on custom CDS—becomes cumbersome.

To address this, I built a small ABAP utility that reads CDS source code directly, extracts downstream dependencies, and filters the output to include only relevant custom CDS views.

 

Objective

The primary objective of this utility is:

  • ✅Identify downstream CDS dependencies
  • ✅Focus only on custom CDS views (Z / ZZ)**
  • ✅Exclude reverse dependencies (association to parent)
  • ✅Provide a clean hierarchical output
  • ✅Offer a reusable tool for large CDS landscapes

Why Not Use Standard Tools?

SAP ADT dependency analysis works well for visualization, but in practice: 

 

Limitation Impact
Includes standard CDS (I_, C_)Noise in analysis
No easy exportHard to analyze further
Limited recursion clarityDifficult tracking of multi-level dependencies
 
This utility was designed specifically to overcome these gaps.
 
Approach

The core idea is simple:

Instead of relying on metadata tables, we parse the actual CDS source code stored in:

DDDDLSRC

This ensures that:

  • We work with the real definition
  • We capture joins and associations accurately
  • We can control what is included or excluded

Sample Output

Level Level Description Parent Child
1Root LevelCDS_ACDS_B
1Root LevelCDS_ACDS_C
2Child Level 1CDS_BCDS_D

✔ Only custom CDS appear
✔ Parent associations are excluded
✔ Hierarchy is clearly visible

Conclusion

CDS dependency graphs in ADT are helpful, but when working on complex models, they often contain too much noise to be actionable.

By parsing CDS source directly and applying targeted filtering, it becomes much easier to:

  • Focus on what truly matters
  • Understand downstream impact
  • Maintain clarity in large data models

A lightweight utility like this can significantly simplify CDS analysis and debugging.

 

Sample Code

 
REPORT z_cds_downstream_dependency.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* SELECTION SCREEN
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_cds TYPE ddlname OBLIGATORY DEFAULT 'Z_CDS_I_ABC'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* TYPES
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_dep,
         level      TYPE i,
         level_desc TYPE string,
         parent     TYPE ddlname,
         child      TYPE ddlname,
       END OF ty_dep.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* DATA
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
DATA: gt_result  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_dep,
      gt_visited TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddlname.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* FORM: GET_DEPENDENCIES (Recursive CDS Source Parser)
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_dependencies
  USING
    iv_parent TYPE ddlname
    iv_level  TYPE i.

  DATA: lt_source     TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddddlsrc,
        ls_source     TYPE ddddlsrc,
        lv_line       TYPE string,
        lv_child      TYPE ddlname,
        lv_next_level TYPE i.

  "---------------------------------------------------------------"
  " Prevent infinite loops
  "---------------------------------------------------------------"
  IF line_exists( gt_visited[ table_line = iv_parent ] ).
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  APPEND iv_parent TO gt_visited.

  "---------------------------------------------------------------"
  " Read CDS source code
  "---------------------------------------------------------------"
  SELECT *
    INTO TABLE lt_source
    FROM ddddlsrc
    WHERE ddlname = iv_parent.

  DATA: lv_prev_token TYPE string.

  LOOP AT lt_source INTO ls_source.

    lv_line = ls_source-source.

    " Normalize line
    TRANSLATE lv_line TO UPPER CASE.

    "-----------------------------------------------------------"
    " Look for dependency keywords
    "-----------------------------------------------------------"
    IF lv_line CS 'FROM'
       OR lv_line CS 'JOIN'
       OR lv_line CS 'ASSOCIATION'.

      DATA: lt_tokens TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF string,
            lv_token  TYPE string.
      DATA lv_desc TYPE string.
      SPLIT lv_line AT space INTO TABLE lt_tokens.
      CLEAR lv_prev_token.
      LOOP AT lt_tokens INTO lv_token.


        "---------------------------------------------------"
        " ✅ Precise skip: ONLY 'TO PARENT <CDS>'
        "---------------------------------------------------"
        IF lv_token = 'PARENT'.
          lv_prev_token = 'PARENT'.
        ENDIF.

        "-------------------------------------------------------"
        " Identify custom CDS (Z*)
        "-------------------------------------------------------"
        IF lv_token CP 'Z*'.

          lv_child = lv_token.

          " Clean unwanted characters
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF ',' IN lv_child WITH ''.
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF '"' IN lv_child WITH ''.
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF '(' IN lv_child WITH ''.
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF ')' IN lv_child WITH ''.
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF '#' IN lv_child WITH ''.
          REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF REGEX '[^A-Z0-9_]' IN lv_child WITH ''.

          " Skip self reference
          IF lv_child = iv_parent.
            CONTINUE.
          ENDIF.

          "---------------------------------------------------"
          " Store result
          "---------------------------------------------------"
          IF lv_prev_token = 'PARENT'.

          ELSE.
            CLEAR lv_desc.

            CASE iv_level.
              WHEN 1.
                lv_desc = 'Root Level'.
              WHEN 2.
                lv_desc = 'Child Level 1'.
              WHEN 3.
                lv_desc = 'Child Level 2'.
              WHEN 4.
                lv_desc = 'Child Level 3'.
              WHEN OTHERS.
                lv_desc = |Child Level { iv_level }|.
            ENDCASE.

            APPEND VALUE ty_dep(
                parent      = iv_parent
                child       = lv_child
                level       = iv_level
                level_desc  = lv_desc ) TO gt_result.

            "---------------------------------------------------"
            " Recursive call
            "---------------------------------------------------"
            lv_next_level = iv_level + 1.

            PERFORM get_dependencies USING lv_child lv_next_level.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* START
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
START-OF-SELECTION.

  PERFORM get_dependencies USING p_cds 1.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Remove duplicates
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
  SORT gt_result BY parent child.
  DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM gt_result COMPARING parent child.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* Sort by hierarchy level
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
  SORT gt_result BY level parent.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* ALV OUTPUT
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

  DATA: lo_alv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.

  TRY.


      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING r_salv_table = lo_alv
        CHANGING  t_table      = gt_result ).

      DATA: lo_functions TYPE REF TO cl_salv_functions.

      lo_functions = lo_alv->get_functions( ).
      lo_functions->set_all( abap_true ).

      lo_alv->get_columns( )->set_optimize( abap_true ).

      TRY.
          DATA(lo_columns) = lo_alv->get_columns( ).

          lo_columns->get_column( 'LEVEL' )->set_short_text( 'Level' ).
          lo_columns->get_column( 'LEVEL_DESC' )->set_medium_text( 'Level Description' ).
          lo_columns->get_column( 'PARENT' )->set_medium_text( 'Parent CDS' ).
          lo_columns->get_column( 'CHILD' )->set_medium_text( 'Child CDS' ).

        CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
      ENDTRY.
      lo_alv->display( ).

    CATCH cx_salv_msg.
  ENDTRY.

 

Final Note

This solution is intentionally kept simple and flexible.

✅You can certainly enhance this further based on your specific requirements.

For example:

  • Add dependency type (JOIN / ASSOCIATION)
  • Integrate performance metrics
  • Build visual dependency trees
  • Export results to external formats

If you have worked on similar utilities or have additional enhancements, it would be great to see how others are approaching CDS dependency analysis.

entry.title: Re: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: Migration to S/4HANA EWM on Private Cloud
entry.content:
Hello jayshree_ravi468834 

As far as standard SAP guidance goes, migration scenarios from Embedded EWM to Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud are generally not covered as a direct system-to-system migration via Migration Cockpit. These scenarios usually require a project-based approach with system landscape redesign and data/process migration planning.

I would recommend checking the latest SAP Notes and release restrictions for your specific S/4HANA version, and also engaging your SAP account team or SAP consulting services for a validated migration approach.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Launchpad Extension v3.2 – Dark Mode, Auto-Login & More
entry.content:
What is the SAP Cloud ERP Launchpad Extension?

 

A quick recap for new readers: this extension (available for Chrome, Edge, and Firefox) lets you save your favourite SAP Fiori apps and Cloud ERP system URLs directly in the browser toolbar. Instead of navigating through bookmarks or typing URLs manually, you open the extension popup, click your app, and you're there. Read more here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/can-a-free-chrome-extension-transform-your-sap-cloud-erp-experience/ba-p/13801414

 

🌙 Dark Mode

The most visually impactful change: the extension now supports a full dark mode, consistent across both the popup and the options page.

To switch it on, open the Options page (click the extension icon → settings, or right-click → "Extension options") and toggle the Dark Mode switch in the footer bar.

 

The dark theme applies to:

- The extension popup (tree view, buttons, footer bar, search field)

- The full Options page (tables, dialogs, tab headers, the shell bar)

Your preference is saved automatically and persists across browser restarts.

 

🔑 Auto-Login with Saved Credentials

You can now save a username and password for each of your Cloud ERP systems. When you navigate to a saved system's SAP Fiori Launchpad for the first time, the extension automatically fills in the login form for you — no typing needed.

How to save credentials

  1. Open the Options page
  2. Go to the Cloud ERP Systems tab
  3. Click Create to add a new system, or select an existing one and click Edit
  4. Fill in the optional Username and Password fields
  5. Click Save

 

The password is stored securely in your browser's sync storage and is shown as "Saved" in the table — the actual value is never displayed again for security.

How auto-login works

 

Once credentials are saved, the extension's background service will detect when you open a matching SAP system URL and automatically inject your username and password into the login form. This works seamlessly in the background — you just arrive at the Fiori Launchpad already logged in.

 

> Note: Credentials are stored only locally in your browser and are never transmitted anywhere. When you export your Cloud ERP Systems list, the download file contains only the name and host URL — credentials are intentionally excluded to protect your privacy.

 

🏢Adding Cloud ERP Systems with Login Credentials Directly from the Popup

The popup now includes Username and Password fields when adding a Cloud ERP System through the Add → Cloud ERP System menu.

 

🔒 Privacy-Safe Export

When you download your Cloud ERP Systems list (Download button in the footer of the Options page, while on the Cloud ERP Systems tab), the exported JSON file now contains only the name and host URL for each system.

Usernames and passwords are never included in exports. This means you can safely shareyour system list with colleagues without worrying about credential exposure.

 

🦊 Firefox: Fixes and Improvements

Several improvements have been made specifically for Firefox users:

- Tree duplication fixed: A Firefox-specific bug caused the Fiori Apps tree in the popup to render items twice. This was caused by Firefox firing browser storage callbacks more than once under certain conditions. The issue has been fully resolved.

- Upload fixed: The file upload (importing JSON) now works correctly in Firefox.

- Translations: All new field labels (Username column, Password column, "Saved" status) are now fully translated across all 18 supported languages in the Firefox version.

 

🔗Download and Install

Chrome:

https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/sap-cloud-erp-launchpad/nelmoakcfgfgkigcjgkmibhmgfpbhcbh

Edge:

https://microsoftedge.microsoft.com/addons/detail/fiori-launchpad-for-sap-c/kfbfaeokdhfbeikhofemebocbbfcmpcb

Firefox:

https://addons.mozilla.org/de/firefox/addon/sap-cloud-erp-launchpad/

 

Documentation:

https://edrilanberisha.github.io/sapclouderplaunchpad/

 

💬 Feedback Welcome

 

As always, this extension is built for the SAP community. If you find bugs, have feature requests, or just want to say the dark mode made your day — please leave a comment below or open an issue on GitHub.

 

Happy navigating! 🚀

entry.title: Re: The SmartRecruiters and SAP SuccessFactors Integration Roadmap
entry.content: We got that same message, not idea what it is about but would be interested to know more

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Columbus - Ohio, July 25th, 2026
entry.content:
First time in Columbus

We are organizing SAP INSIDE TRACK in Columbus, Ohio, for the first time. The main reason for organizing this event is to continue our community awareness in the Ohio State area; hopefully, we can grow it into other cities and perhaps other states.

If you like to learn and network, and if you work in S/4HANA, BTP, AI, Fiori, functional and technical sides or any other SAP area, then this event is for you. We will also have food and drinks at the event as well. Don't miss the opportunity to participate. See you there.


Why Attend?

Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just beginning your SAP journey, SAP Inside Track Columbus offers something for everyone:

  • Learn from SAP experts, practitioners, and community leaders
  • Explore the latest developments across the SAP ecosystem
  • Discover real-world customer stories and implementation experiences
  • Network with SAP professionals from across the Midwest region
  • Engage in discussions around emerging technologies and best practices
  • Meet fellow community members and expand your professional network

Agenda

Time (in EST)SpeakerDescription
9:00 AM SIT Columbus Logo and Tagline unveil
9:15 AMPawan KhatriERP Implementation scope in USA
10:15 AMManish GargSCM in Pharma Industry
11:00 AM Tea and Snacks Break
11:15 AMVaibhav MishraIntegrating AI-Based Predictive Maintenance with SAP S4HANA
12:15 PMLeela Vinod CudapahFrom Consultants to AI-Orchestrators: Transforming SAP Implementation Ways of Working for the AI Era
1:00 PM Lunch (sponsored by eBusiness Solutions Inc.)
2:00 PMPriyanka MishraPhase-based Maintenance in SAP S4HANA Asset Management. - Challenges and Case Studies
3:00 PMRajiv ShahSAP FICO in SAP S4HANA


Our Sponsor
This event is sponsored by eBusiness Solutions Inc. eBusiness Solutions provided the meeting rooms, breakout area, and lunch for this event. 

Location

200 E Campus View Blvd STE 200, Columbus, OH 43235

Parking
Free parking is available at the location. We can easily accommodate more than 30 four-wheelers.

Globally
The event is held in several countries around the world. Its organizers are active members of the SAP Community to share knowledge and strengthen professionals among the participants.
The event has been held in several cities around the world for over 15 years.

Learn more about how to hold your SAP Inside Track event in your city through this LINK.

entry.title: Re: Planned Delivery Cost of Purchasing in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
@Qi_Liu 

Thank you very much, Qi Liu.

We will look into the API option after the initial data migration. We will also submit this as product feedback to SAP for consideration in a future release.

Thank you!

Wei

entry.title: Error : Unknown Account Assignment not defined for use here
entry.content:
Hi, During the creation of Release order with reference to contract using BAPI PO Create1 the error : 'Unknown Account Assignment not defined for use here' is displayed. 

The contract is with AAC ( Account Assignment category) -U. During creation of release order the AAC is changed from U to K and the other mandatory field values GL code and Cost Center is entered. However the error is displayed.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Identity Services Bug in SAP BTP Trial Environment: Current Status
entry.content: @Any updates ??

entry.title: the inspection of Intercompany Process for Customer Returns (2F4) can be automated processing
entry.content:
Hi,

In the Intercompany Process for Customer Returns, I have to use the scope item 2F4. 

When I post the Returns Delivery , the system will create the inspection document , I have to confirm the inspection document .

I need to receive inventory, but do not require quality inspection confirmation on the system.

The inspection document can be setted to skip ?  Or are  there development interfaces available for automated processing ?

entry.title: Re: From Prototype to Production with Joule Studio: Recap of Session 4 of Architecting the AE Webina
entry.content:
@Gaurish-Dessai - How to playaround with Joule Work, Joule Studio, Joule Desktop..... ?

If this possible via BTP Trail account... Many of us can understand better and infuse it into our SAP Application... Since current its buzz word and no hands on it on how it works....

Kindly plan to introduce on how others can leverage more

entry.title: Joule Work on RISE — do SAP GUI users need to move to Fiori, or only for the embedded Joule Assistan
entry.content:
We're a RISE with SAP (S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition) customer, and most users still work in classic SAP GUI, not Fiori.

My understanding is that the embedded Joule Assistant is surfaced inside the UI, so that would require Fiori — but Joule Work is a separate, standalone agentic workspace that executes against the backend via
APIs, independent of the user's transactional screen.

So my question is: for Joule Work specifically, can our SAP GUI users keep using GUI as-is, or do they need to migrate to Fiori?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Value Mapping Migration from SAP PI to SAP CPI
entry.content: Consider creating a support ticket and provide all technical details.

entry.title: Re: Which SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Apps Are Required for...
entry.content: Thank you for your answer. I really appreciate that. Have a nice day !

Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 19 - CDS source formatter

entry.title: Re: SAP guide for release pipeline for SAP IS to KAFKA
entry.content:
Is this by chance what you need?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/sap-cpi-ci-cd-from-from-zero-to-hero/ba-p/13543950

entry.title: Re: Reprocessing Retry Messages in SAP CPI
entry.content: This seems to be expected behavior, with intentionally designed retry mechanism.

entry.title: Re: Azure OAuth Integration Failure
entry.content:
Refer to:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cloud-integration-send-mail-via-microsoft-graph-api-with-oauth-2-0/ba-p/13549710

You must have missed something on MS side.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: Roles For Adding Capabilities in CPI System
entry.content:
Role collection: Integration_Provisioner

For full steps, see:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/integration-suite-simplified-onboarding-and-provisioning/ba-p/13446258

entry.title: Re: GCS JWT token generation - SAP CPI neo - PKIX path bu...
entry.content:
oauth2.googleapis.com => GlobalSign Root CA

Is this trusted?

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite Service Plan Issue
entry.content:
Per your description, you have different service plans:

  • Prod: Standard
  • Test: successfactors_edition

There are some features available since higher plans like Standard, and they can only be available when you upgrade your Test to a higher service plan.

entry.title: Re: BTP Destination creation error
entry.content: If you can't even create (save) a destination, open a support ticket with BC-CP-DEST-CF.

entry.title: Re: High Sea Sales
entry.content: Great Answer !

entry.title: Re: DTW does not show list of companies after enabling logging on with Current Domain User
entry.content: What is the isssue ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Q&A
entry.content: Yes, you are at the right place

entry.title: S4HC | Batch Check Printing
entry.content:
Hi Sir/Ma'am,

Hi Sir/Ma'am,

We would like to ask for your assistance on what scope items are needed to be activated for Batch Check Printing if this is standard functionality in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, as well as any documentation, if ever.

Thank you.

 

Sincerely,

Melvin Banggo

entry.title: Aging of Revenue / VAT
entry.content:
Hi Sir/Ma'am,

Our client has this specific requirement and we want to ask for your assistance if there is standard functionality available for this in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and what are the workarounds, if ever.

Requirement:

Instead of the usual aging of the receivables, the client also age the VAT and Revenue lines of the sales transaction.  They generate reports based on which VAT and revenue accounts/lines are still outstanding and how old are they.

We greatly appreciate your assistance on this concern.

 

Thank you very much and God bless.

Sincerely,


Melvin Banggo

entry.title: Re: Worflow inbox - Error when starting submethod
entry.content: The solution was described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/error-when-starting-submethod/qaa-p/14426436/highlight/true#M4944672

entry.title: Re: Extra objects being populated in the Calibration PM O...
entry.content:
Greetings,

check if you have additional objects assigned to your maintenance plan item. Use transaction IP03. You will find maintenance plan number in planning tab of your maintenance order. 

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: Partner user cannot see Partnership section in Sap4Me
entry.content: In order to get the full benefits of SAP for Me, ensure that you contact your Partner User Administrator (previously called Partner Security Manager) to provide you with the permissions you need for your specific role. If you do not know who is your Partner User Administrator, just check SAP for Me > My Important Contacts To get further help, you may refer to the attachment to use get support channel to contact the responsible partner support team. Please also see this KBA for more info: 3165784 - How to get assistance for Partner Dashboards and the related partner cards - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: creating a follow-on HU Warehouse Task after Warehous...
entry.content:
Hi @KGANTI2022 ,

creation of Warhouse Task  as follow up inside /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_POST is a common way. Inside the Badi Implementation you should call qRFC  for example /SCWM/TO_CREATE_MOVE_HU with prefix WMTH.

entry.title: Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standard role for role cat. MKK
entry.content:
When creating a sales order using the Domestic Account Group (ZDOM), I receive the following error:

"Business partner 100231 does not exist in the standard role for role category MKK."

However, when I create a sales order using the Foreign Account Group (ZFRN), the sales order is created successfully without any issues.

I have already compared the backend configuration for both account groups, and the configuration appears to be the same. Despite this, the error occurs only for ZDOM.

Could you please help identify what might be causing this issue or advise which additional configuration or master data should be checked?

entry.title: Re: Recording one characteristic at multiple fixed measur...
entry.content:
Greetings,

have you tried using a different sampling procedure for the "special" characteristics? In example below for the first two characteristcs only one result is required and fo the last one 5 results need to be entered. This is managed by sampling procedure which is assigned to inspection characteristic on the ionspection plan.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Summary
entry.content:
Well, it's the end of the month, which means time for a wrap up of the Developer Challenge we've just had. It's been a lot of fun for us to create the questions and see how much you've all participated, and, from your comments, how much you've also enjoyed it. Thank you for taking part!

As you know, there are no prizes, the only reward is increased knowledge and understanding of a fundamentally important technology in the SAP developer universe. We'd love to hear from you (in comments to this post) what you thought, how you found the questions, whether you'd like to see something this again, and anything else that occurs to you.

Half way through the month, we published a brief analysis of your progress and success on the questions so far, and continued to update that on a daily basis. We've just run a final analysis update today (Sunday 28 June), which should have given you all enough time to answer the final question which was published at the beginning of last Friday.

 

Facts, figures and heroes

Over the 20 questions you all submitted a total of 753 responses, which is fab! Thank you to everyone who did submit at least one response.

Bronze

In fact, here's a list of what we're going to call "Bronze" winners - everyone who participated:

AakankshaWs AfshanAnsari Andrii Anusha2025 Caisi Chaimae_Chairi27 Dan_Wroblewski FlorianP1 GauravPhadnis Greltel Guru_Charan HarshPatelV Henning Ihor_Haranichev JFenris Jawaharch Joery Laco M-Uzi MatLakaemper MattHarding Mayank_Mittal8 MioYasutake MohamedAbdelghany NakulG NeelimaEmmani NiklausB PVSBPrasadK PrabhuDC RajeshOlive Raphael Rupesh_Kumar25 SSaiSanthosh SachinArtani SergioG_TX Sushakude Swathi_SAP_Philomath SyamsundarKarukola VishnAndr YLN Yogananda _Jakub_ aelghannam ajmaradiaga ankitsrivastav aryan-11 berkaydurmaz berkayergun cayotteRizing ditt_kalyan furkan5 hrushikeshraju_yerram juancarlos_dasilva kasch-code lidavidlee mhameister milan_10 mvaibhav nagarjundas_1994 omer_sakar parthasaradhi sarvesh_agarwal satya01 shotokka shubhamkar soheil_st soisses stickman_0x00 sudarshan_b tchaitanya tobiasz_h

(Sorry we're not able to tag you all, the blogging platform limits the number of users one can mention in a single post to just 20).

Silver

There's a special subset of these participants who provided a response to every single question, well done and thank you for your perseverance and tenacity!

- @SachinArtani
- @shotokka
- @kasch-code
- @Swathi_SAP_Philomath
- @MatLakaemper
- @parthasaradhi
- @Dan_Wroblewski
- @Laco
- @ajmaradiaga
- @Anusha2025
- @VishnAndr
- @Joery
- @lidavidlee
- @Andrii
- @MioYasutake
- @berkaydurmaz
- @mvaibhav
- @Rupesh_Kumar25
- @furkan5

Gold

And there's an elite subset of the silver subset who not only provided a response to every single question, but got them all correct, amazing work, thank you and well done indeed! 🚀

- @SachinArtani
- @shotokka
- @kasch-code
- @MatLakaemper
- @parthasaradhi
- @Dan_Wroblewski
- @mvaibhav
- @furkan5

Wrapping up

Well, that just about wraps it up for this month's Developer Challenge. We hope you had fun, and learned something. Let us know also in the comments what you think of the CAP documentation that you've been exploring and studying over the last few weeks. We (and the CAP documentation folks) would love to know.

We'll keep the results OData v4 service online and available for the next few days, in case you want to do some analysis of your own. 

Thanks everyone!

entry.title: The status of item category SVS4 in the Service Order cannot be set to "Completed".
entry.content:
Hi Expert！

I use Service Order in the Public Cloud.
Material A is registered with the item category group SVBL, and Material B with SRVM.
I registered the service order as follows.

ItemParentServiceOrderItemproductitem categorystatus
10 ASVB4Released
2010BSVS4Released
     

When the Service Order is released, a Purchase Requisition is created for Item 20.
Subsequently, a Purchase Order was created and a Goods Receipt was posted.

When I changed the status in the Service Order header to "Completed," only the status of Item 10 changed to "Completed," while the status of Item 20 remained "Released."

ItemParentServiceOrderItemproductitem categorystatus
10 ASVB4Completed
2010BSVS4Released
     

How do I change the status of Item 20 to "Completed"?

Soma
